text: Improve discoverability of uncommitted aims (so the autonomous loop finds all open work, not just phase-committed)
description: Found via Felix's hunch: 60 of 81 open aims (74%) are not committed to any phase yet all have parents, so they were invisible to every discovery path — get_prioritized_aims only ranks phase-committed aims, and the `floating` filter requires no-phase AND no-parents. Fixed in commit ca20f0e: added an `uncommitted` filter (backend aim.list + MCP list_aims), an `uncommittedOpen` section in graph_hygiene, and pointed get_prioritized_aims/list_aims/graph_hygiene descriptions at it + commit_aim_to_phase. Remaining: triage the existing ~60 uncommitted open aims into phases (judgment per aim — left for incremental/human triage now that they're findable).

reflections.context: Felix's hunch that uncommitted aims weren't being returned by the loop's discovery tools. The prioritized-aims loop kept surfacing only vague container aims.
reflections.outcome: Proved with data: 60/81 open aims (74%) are unphased-but-parented — invisible because get_prioritized_aims needs a phase and the floating filter needs no-parents. Added an `uncommitted` filter + graph_hygiene uncommittedOpen section + description nudges (ca20f0e, tested).
reflections.effectiveness: High: a single missing filter dimension was hiding three-quarters of open work from an autonomous loop whose whole premise is finding work. Cheap fix, large unblock.
reflections.lesson: When an autonomous loop 'runs out of work', suspect the DISCOVERY query before concluding the backlog is empty. Two near-synonymous concepts (no-phase vs no-parents) were conflated in one filter, creating a blind spot exactly where most aims live.

status.comment: Tooling shipped in ca20f0e with tests. Backlog triage of the ~60 existing uncommitted aims is now possible via list_aims uncommitted=true / graph_hygiene uncommittedOpen.
